title: Tribunals cannot close cases with cryptic orders, they must give reasons: Kerala High Court
description: The Kerala High Court recently set aside an order passed by a tribunal for Local Self Government Institutions on finding that it was cryptically worded and did not record any reasons for its conclusions [Renjini KK v Mannancherry Grama Panchayat & ors].Justice PV Kunhikrishnan emphasised that tribunals must pass speaking orders recording reasons for its conclusions. He observed that tribunals cannot simply narrate facts and then close the matter with a few lines without discussing the rival
